# Fortune Cat

> Fortune Cat is a web-based personal expense tracker that fills itself: users forward the
> transaction SMS and emails their bank already sends, and Fortune Cat parses them into a live
> cash-flow ledger. It never asks for a bank login and parsing happens on the user's device.

Site: https://fortune-cat-nu.vercel.app

## What it does

- Automatic transaction capture from forwarded bank SMS and emails (and pasted statements).
  The first message from a new sender waits in a review queue; once the sender is trusted,
  later transactions post automatically.
- Manual expense/income logging with categories also works — no SMS or email required.
- Running balance, monthly cash-flow, category breakdown, and a daily "fortune slip": a
  deterministic reading computed from the user's real spending, including a concrete
  spend target for the day.
- Works worldwide: users pick their country at onboarding (33 countries listed, more on
  request), and all amounts display in their own currency. Foreign-currency captures are
  converted into the user's base currency at ECB reference rates.

## Pricing

- Free tier: manual + automatic logging, one capture inbox, last 10 transactions of history.
- Pro: US$9 one-time payment (not a subscription — no renewal, no monthly fee). Unlocks
  Safe-to-Spend, savings goals, recurring-bill radar, subscription kill-chain with cancel
  playbooks, deep analytics, actionable daily readings, full transaction history, and
  three capture inboxes.

## Privacy and security

- No bank account connection, no bank credentials, no screen-scraping. Fortune Cat only
  sees the notification text the user chooses to forward.
- Each user's data is isolated with database row-level security. Users can export their
  transactions as CSV or delete their account (and all data) at any time from Settings.

## Key pages

- Home: https://fortune-cat-nu.vercel.app/
- Pro pricing: https://fortune-cat-nu.vercel.app/upgrade
- Sign up (free): https://fortune-cat-nu.vercel.app/signup
